content.description:
The Silent Update pattern solves a fundamental tension in Hobson: saving data to the database normally triggers a re-render, but during user interactions like dragging, re-rendering destroys the DOM and breaks the interaction mid-flight.

## The Problem

Hobson's reactive rendering means any call to `api.update(item)` triggers a full re-render of the affected item. This is usually desirable — when you edit a note, you want to see the change. But during continuous interactions:

- **Dragging a window** generates position updates on every mouse move
- **Resizing** generates size updates continuously
- **Z-index changes** when clicking to bring a window to front

If each of these triggered a re-render, the DOM would be destroyed and recreated mid-drag, losing:
- The drag interaction itself
- Scroll positions within windows
- Focus state (which element has focus)
- Text selection ranges
- Textarea cursor positions

## The Solution: Two-Tier Save

```javascript
// Normal update — saves AND re-renders
await api.update(item);

// Silent update — saves WITHOUT re-rendering
await api.set(item);
```

`api.set()` writes to IndexedDB but does not trigger a re-render. `api.update()` writes AND re-renders. The calling code chooses which to use based on context.

## When to Use Each

| Scenario | Method | Why |
|----------|--------|-----|
| Position updates during drag | `api.set()` | DOM must survive |
| Z-index change (click to front) | `api.set()` | Only need `style.zIndex` change |
| Resize during handle drag | `api.set()` | DOM must survive |
| Adding/removing an attachment | `api.update()` | New DOM element needed |
| Changing item content | `api.update()` | Content must re-render |
| Changing item type | `api.update()` | May need different view |
| View config update (axis, layout) | `silent: true` | View config is metadata — must not trigger parent re-render |

## The Trade-off

Silent updates preserve DOM state but create the [Stale Closure](item://c0c0c0c0-0011-0000-0000-000000000000) problem. After a silent update, the database has current state but JavaScript closures from render time hold old state. Event handlers must read fresh data from the database.

## Implementation Notes

The pattern applies to the kernel's storage/rendering split:
- `kernel.storage.set()` — pure storage, no side effects
- `kernel.rendering.renderItem()` — triggers render cycle
- `api.update()` combines both; `api.set()` is storage-only

Views that use silent updates should also update the DOM directly for immediate visual feedback (e.g., setting `wrapper.style.left` during drag), then persist the final state on mouse-up.

## See Also

- [Stale Closure Prevention](item://c0c0c0c0-0011-0000-0000-000000000000) — The companion pattern for reading fresh data
- [Scroll State Preservation](item://c0c0c0c0-0012-0000-0000-000000000000) — Saving/restoring scroll across re-renders
- [Spatial Windowing](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) — Primary consumer of this pattern
